# Active-tier re-chunk never persists ŌĆö discarded by the data-sync

## Context

Follow-up to #13328 / #13332. #13332 added `reconcileActiveChunks` (the active-tier ordinal-100 rebalance) to heal the portal's chunk drift, and the portal **index** numbers are now correct on dev. But while advancing the L4 portal-render verification residual @neo-gpt flagged on the #13332 review (`PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABDAicQw`), a V-B-A on `origin/dev` showed the **content** is still drifted: the re-chunk's renames never persist.

## The Problem

`reconcileActiveChunks` is wired **only** into `buildScripts/docs/rebuildContentIndexesAndSeo.mjs` ŌĆö the index-rebuild step the hourly `data-sync-pipeline.yml` runs. That workflow's commit step stages **only** `apps/portal/resources/data` + SEO (`sitemap.xml`, `llms.txt`) and then `git reset --hard` (`.github/workflows/data-sync-pipeline.yml:73`) **discards** every other working-tree change ŌĆö including the re-chunk's `resources/content/**` renames.

Net effect (V-B-A'd on `origin/dev`):
- The **index** is regenerated from the re-chunked working tree and committed ŌåÆ portal nav numbers are correct (tickets `active-chunk-1..4` = 100/100/100/15; pulls `active-chunk-1..2`).
- The underlying **content** is reset ŌåÆ `resources/content/issues` stays ordinal-drifted (chunk distribution ~75/89/100/29/9/5/2/1/1/2/3, not 100/100/100/15).
- ŌåÆ index Ōåö content **desync** (deep-link / `_index.json` lookups resolve against the re-chunked layout the committed content no longer matches), and the re-chunk **re-runs-and-discards every hour, never converging**.

The data-sync pipeline is not the content-commit owner. `resources/content/**` is committed out-of-band by `SyncService` (the "chore: ticket sync [skip ci]" and "Content Sync" commits).

## The Architectural Reality

- `ai/services/github-workflow/SyncService.mjs` is the content-commit owner. It already runs the **sibling reconciles** before committing: `IssueSyncer.reconcileClosedIssueLocations` (`:130`) and `PullRequestSyncer.reconcileClosedPullRequestLocations` (`:136`) ŌĆö the activeŌåÆarchive transition for closed items.
- It does **not** run the active-tier ordinal rebalance. So after a delta sync drifts the active chunks, nothing in the content-commit path rebalances them; only the (discarded) index-rebuild does.
- `reconcileActiveChunks` (`ai/services/github-workflow/shared/reconcileActiveChunks.mjs`) is idempotent (re-running on already-correct content is a no-op), so it is safe to call from more than one site.

## The Fix

Call `reconcileActiveChunks` in `SyncService`, **after** the closed-location reconciles (`:130`/`:136`) and **before** the content-commit, for issues + pulls + discussions. The active-tier rebalance then lands in the "ticket sync" / "Content Sync" commit (the content-commit path), so the content converges to the ordinal-100 layout and stays in sync with the index.

Keep the existing `rebuildContentIndexesAndSeo` call as **index-side defense** (idempotent; keeps the index correct even if a data-sync runs before the next SyncService pass).

## Decision Record impact

`aligned-with` ADR 0004 (universal ordinal-100 chunking) ŌĆö enforces the ADR's active-tier invariant in the content-commit path, where it was missing.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `SyncService` calls `reconcileActiveChunks` for issues, pulls, and discussions after the closed-location reconciles and before the content-commit.
- [ ] A unit test asserts the active re-chunk runs in the sync path (seam/spy on `reconcileActiveChunks`, asserted in the SyncService sync sequence) ŌĆö mirroring how the existing closed-location reconciles are covered.
- [ ] After a sync that drifts active chunks, the committed `resources/content/{issues,pulls,discussions}` converges to the ordinal-100 layout (each active chunk = 100 except the last).
- [ ] The `rebuildContentIndexesAndSeo` call site is retained (index-side defense).

## Out of Scope

- The L4 portal-render verification residual itself (the #13332 review follow-up) ŌĆö this ticket fixes the content-side cause; render verification stays its own task.
- Prod deploy freshness / Cloud Run re-deploy automation (#12964) ŌĆö separate axis.
- Fixing the delta-syncers to not drift in the first place (the reconcile is the post-hoc heal by design).
- Making the data-sync-pipeline.yml commit-staging stage `resources/content` (rejected: a second committer of `resources/content` collides with SyncService's ownership + the workflow's `git reset --hard`/rebase model).

## Related

- Parent: #13328 (portal chunk drift) ŌåÆ #13332 (the index-side re-chunk, merged).
- Sibling follow-up: the #13332 review residual ŌĆö L4 portal-render verification (`PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABDAicQw`).
- #12964 ŌĆö middleware SSR rebuild + Cloud Run deploy automation (deploy axis).
- ADR 0004 ŌĆö universal ordinal-100 chunking.

- Retrieval Hint (semantic): "active-tier re-chunk discarded by data-sync reset; SyncService content-commit owner"
- Retrieval Hint (commit anchors): #13332 head `f1d723a20f0e95c8e1cf572e649f44872a2381d1`; `.github/workflows/data-sync-pipeline.yml:73` (the `git reset --hard`); `SyncService.mjs:130/136` (the sibling reconciles).

Live latest-open sweep: checked latest 25 open issues at 2026-06-15 (~13:35Z) + A2A claim sweep (30 recent) + `git grep origin/dev`; no equivalent ticket or in-flight claim found. KB semantic sweep unavailable (collections error) ŌĆö live + A2A + grep sweeps sufficient.

Release classification: boardless (v13 shipped; data-pipeline correctness, not release-gating).

## Timeline

### @neo-claude-opus - 2026-06-15T13:45:49Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code):**

> ŌØŗ **Retracting ŌĆö false premise (caught pre-implementation).**
> 
> The core claim ŌĆö "`reconcileActiveChunks` never reaches the content-commit path; wire it into `SyncService`" ŌĆö is wrong. `SyncService.runFullSync` **already** runs the re-chunk and commits the content:
> 
> - `SyncService.mjs:180` ŌåÆ `await this.rebuildContentIndexesAndSeo()` ŌåÆ runs `reconcileActiveChunks` for issues/pulls/discussions (the exact call site #13332 added).
> - `SyncService.mjs:200`/`:211` stage + commit `generatedSyncStatusPaths`, which **includes** `resources/content/issues/`, `pulls/`, `discussions/`, and `_index.json` (`SyncService.mjs:16-27`).
> - `pushToRepoAfterSync: leaf(true)` is the live default ŌĆö the commit path is active.
> 
> So the re-chunk reaches the content-commit owner transitively (via `rebuildContentIndexesAndSeo`); it is **not** mis-wired. The "add a `SyncService` call" fix this ticket proposed already exists.
> 
> **Why the content is currently drifted (benign, self-healing):** the last `chore: ticket sync` (the SyncService content-commit) ran `2026-06-14 21:21` ŌĆö ~16h *before* #13332 merged (`2026-06-15 14:02`). No sync_all has run since the merge; only the hourly `data-sync-pipeline.yml`, which by design re-chunks the index but discards `resources/content` (it is not the content-commit owner). The next sync_all runs the now-re-chunk-bearing rebuild and commits the converged content. The original portal nav-number bug is already fixed via the committed index.
> 
> **Root of the error:** I traced `reconcileActiveChunks`'s direct caller (`rebuildContentIndexesAndSeo`) and checked only one of its two consumers (the data-sync pipeline, which discards content), concluding "mis-wired" ŌĆö without checking that `SyncService` is the *other* consumer and *does* commit content. Reading `SyncService` whole before editing falsified the premise. No code change is warranted; closing as not planned.
> 
> (Residual that stays valid: the #13332 L4 portal-render / deep-link verification ŌĆö a verification task, not this content-fix.)
